누가복음 14:1.content: When he went into the house of one of the rulers of the Pharisees on a Sabbath to eat bread, they were watching him.

누가복음 14:2.content: Behold, a certain man who had dropsy was in front of him.

누가복음 14:3.content: Jesus, answering, spoke to the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?”

누가복음 14:4.content: But they were silent. He took him, and healed him, and let him go.

누가복음 14:5.content: He answered them, “Which of you, if your son or an ox fell into a well, wouldn’t immediately pull him out on a Sabbath day?”

누가복음 14:6.content: They couldn’t answer him regarding these things.

누가복음 14:7.content: He spoke a parable to those who were invited, when he noticed how they chose the best seats, and said to them,

누가복음 14:8.content: “When you are invited by anyone to a wedding feast, don’t sit in the best seat, since perhaps someone more honorable than you might be invited by him,

누가복음 14:9.content: and he who invited both of you would come and tell you, ‘Make room for this person.’ Then you would begin, with shame, to take the lowest place.

누가복음 14:10.content: But when you are invited, go and sit in the lowest place, so that when he who invited you comes, he may tell you, ‘Friend, move up higher.’ Then you will be honored in the presence of all who sit at the table with you.

누가복음 14:11.content: For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.”

누가복음 14:12.content: He also said to the one who had invited him, “When you make a dinner or a supper, don’t call your friends, nor your brothers, nor your kinsmen, nor rich neighbors, or perhaps they might also return the favor, and pay you back.

누가복음 14:13.content: But when you make a feast, ask the poor, the maimed, the lame, or the blind;

누가복음 14:14.content: and you will be blessed, because they don’t have the resources to repay you. For you will be repaid in the resurrection of the righteous.”

누가복음 14:15.content: When one of those who sat at the table with him heard these things, he said to him, “Blessed is he who will feast in God’s Kingdom!”

누가복음 14:16.content: But he said to him, “A certain man made a great supper, and he invited many people.

누가복음 14:17.content: He sent out his servant at supper time to tell those who were invited, ‘Come, for everything is ready now.’

누가복음 14:18.content: They all as one began to make excuses. “The first said to him, ‘I have bought a field, and I must go and see it. Please have me excused.’

누가복음 14:19.content: “Another said, ‘I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I must go try them out. Please have me excused.’

누가복음 14:20.content: “Another said, ‘I have married a wife, and therefore I can’t come.’

누가복음 14:21.content: “That servant came, and told his lord these things. Then the master of the house, being angry, said to his servant, ‘Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in the poor, maimed, blind, and lame.’

누가복음 14:22.content: “The servant said, ‘Lord, it is done as you commanded, and there is still room.’

누가복음 14:23.content: “The lord said to the servant, ‘Go out into the highways and hedges, and compel them to come in, that my house may be filled.

누가복음 14:24.content: For I tell you that none of those men who were invited will taste of my supper.’ ”

누가복음 14:25.content: Now great multitudes were going with him. He turned and said to them,

누가복음 14:26.content: “If anyone comes to me, and doesn’t disregard his own father, mother, wife, children, brothers, and sisters, yes, and his own life also, he can’t be my disciple.

누가복음 14:27.content: Whoever doesn’t bear his own cross, and come after me, can’t be my disciple.

누가복음 14:28.content: For which of you, desiring to build a tower, doesn’t first sit down and count the cost, to see if he has enough to complete it?

누가복음 14:29.content: Or perhaps, when he has laid a foundation, and is not able to finish, everyone who sees begins to mock him,

누가복음 14:30.content: saying, ‘This man began to build, and wasn’t able to finish.’

누가복음 14:31.content: Or what king, as he goes to encounter another king in war, will not sit down first and consider whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him who comes against him with twenty thousand?

누가복음 14:32.content: Or else, while the other is yet a great way off, he sends an envoy, and asks for conditions of peace.

누가복음 14:33.content: So therefore whoever of you who doesn’t renounce all that he has, he can’t be my disciple.

누가복음 14:34.content: Salt is good, but if the salt becomes flat and tasteless, with what do you season it?

누가복음 14:35.content: It is fit neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. It is thrown out. He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”
